content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of integers of size NNN. For every window size from 111 to NNN, you have to find the maximum value among the minimum(s) of that window size in the array.

For example if A=[2,4,6]A = [2,4,6]A=[2,4,6] then

Windows of size 111 are - [2],[4],[6][2], [4], [6][2],[4],[6], and the minimum value in each window is 2,4,62,4,62,4,6. Among these values, the maximum is 666.

Similarly, windows of size 222 are - [2,4],[4,6][2,4], [4,6][2,4],[4,6], and the minimum value in each window is 2,42,42,4. Among these values, the maximum is 444.

Similarly, Windows of size 333 are - [2,4,6][2,4,6][2,4,6], the minimum value in each window is 222. Among these values, the maximum is 222.

So the answer for this array will be [6,4,2][6,4,2][6,4,2].


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the number of days
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,..,AN‚Äč denoting the integers in the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line NNN integers- the iii-th integer denotes the answer for the window of size iii.
